creator: rakuco@FreeBSD.org
text:
Your approach is valid: if I link amarok_collection-mysqlecollection.so with -Wl,--no-undefined, I see several errors coming from libmysqld.a referencing SSL_*, EVP_* and MD5_* symbols that are not found.

However, another approach is to generalize this check: https://cgit.kde.org/amarok.git/tree/CMakeLists.txt?h=v2.8.0#n77

    if(${CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME} MATCHES "Linux")
        SET(CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS "${CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS} -Wl,--as-needed")
    endif(${CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME} MATCHES "Linux")

which was added in https://cgit.kde.org/amarok.git/commit/CMakeLists.txt?h=v2.8.0&id=ef0d251381532902cbd81bfa7732358be3cecedb for some similar reason apparently.

Amarok doesn't seem to need or use any of those missing symbols, but I haven't tried running it after building everything with --as-needed.

Either approach is fine with me.
